from collections import Counter
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import itertools

def flatten(nested_list):
    return list(itertools.chain.from_iterable(nested_list))

def task_func(list_of_menuitems, title="Menu Distribution", color="blue", width=1.0):
    # Flatten the nested list
    menu_items = flatten(list_of_menuitems)

    # Count the occurrences of each item
    item_counts = Counter(menu_items)

    # Create a sorted list of items for the x-axis
    sorted_x_axis = sorted(item_counts.keys())

    # Prepare data for the histogram
    y_data = [item_counts[item] for item in sorted_x_axis]
    x_data = np.arange(len(sorted_x_axis))

    # Create a new figure and axis object
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()

    # Plot the histogram
    ax.barh(x_data, y_data, color=color, width=width)

    # Set plot labels and title
    ax.set_xlabel("Frequency")
    ax.set_ylabel("Menu Items")
    ax.set_title(title)

    # Return the Axes object and the item_counts dictionary
    return ax, dict(item_counts)
